findings.description: The NC Water Resources logo is rendered slightly differently in the HTML version but is present and correctly placed.
findings.evidence: PDF shows the logo with specific styling in the upper left; HTML shows same logo but with slightly different proportions.

findings.description: The header information (PAT MCCRORY, Governor, DONALD R. VAN DER VAART, Secretary, S. JAY ZIMMERMAN, Director) is right-aligned in the PDF but left-aligned in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF: Header names are right-aligned on the page. HTML: Same names appear left-aligned below the logo and address.

findings.description: The PDF has a two-part layout with the logo on the left and official names on the right; the HTML renders these in a single-column stacked layout.
findings.evidence: PDF: Logo left, names right. HTML: Logo top, address below, names below that.

findings.description: The MEMORANDUM heading in the PDF is underlined; in the HTML it appears underlined as well but with a colored horizontal rule beneath it.
findings.evidence: PDF: simple underlined text. HTML: underlined text with a red/colored rule below.

findings.description: The To/From/Subject fields in the PDF use a tabular-like alignment with labels and values; the HTML renders them as flowing text with colons.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'To:' and 'From:' are left-column labels with values indented. HTML: similar but slightly less structured.

findings.description: Section headings (I, II, III, IV) use Roman numerals with bold formatting in both versions, but the HTML uses larger heading styles compared to the PDF's more modest bold formatting.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'I. History/Background' in bold regular size. HTML: Same text rendered as a larger heading element.

findings.description: Bullet point category labels (Agriculture, Air Quality, etc.) appear with underlined bold formatting in the PDF but bold-only without underline in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF: underlined bold labels like 'Agriculture', 'Air Quality'. HTML: bold labels without underline.

findings.description: In the Written Public Comment Summary section, bullet labels use underlined bold in PDF but bold-only in HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'Beneficial Reuse', 'Costs', etc. are underlined and bold. HTML: same labels are bold only.

findings.description: The document uses h1 for 'MEMORANDUM' title and h2 for sections I, II, III, IV. This is acceptable. However, there are multiple <main> elements in the HTML which is structurally incorrect but does not affect content fidelity.
findings.evidence: HTML contains multiple <main> elements (one per page section), which is non-standard HTML but does not alter content.

findings.description: The page header information (State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Water Resources, address, phone, and official names) appears in both the page-header div and could be considered duplicated from the PDF's single header, but this reflects the PDF's original layout.
findings.evidence: Header block appears once in the HTML page-header div, matching the PDF's single appearance on page 1.

findings.description: Alt text for the logo image ('NC Water Resources Environmental Quality logo') and decorative bar ('Decorative color bar') are reasonable descriptions but are AI-generated additions not present in the PDF. These are acceptable additions.
findings.evidence: alt='NC Water Resources Environmental Quality logo' and alt='Decorative color bar'

findings.description: In the oral comments summary under 'Agriculture', the PDF has an em-dash style connection 'Agriculture–' (no space before dash), which the HTML preserves faithfully. Minor formatting difference but text is accurate.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'Agriculture– One citizen commented...' HTML: 'Agriculture– One citizen commented...'
